title: Transporting Hand-Woven Tibeb Fabrics and Traditional Attire: Zero-Dust Packing Methods

content:
Transporting hand-woven tibeb fabrics and traditional attire: zero-dust packing methods

Hand-woven tibeb and ceremonial attire snag on rough cardboard and grey dust Addis corridors embed in weave—zero-dust packing uses garment bags, acid-free tissue, and sealed wardrobe box vertical not stuffed sack.

This guide transports tibeb fabrics and traditional attire with zero-dust packing methods.

Zero-dust packing stack

  • Clean garments press light steam before pack.
  • Acid-free tissue between folds.
  • Breathable garment bag not plastic long-term sweat.
  • Wardrobe box vertical hanger bar.
  • Outer box sealed tape dust flap.

Tibeb edge protection

Embroidery face tissue pillow; no rubber band direct on weave; roll large netela around tube optional; separate from shoes leather.

Truck placement

Cab or top stack dry zone; away spice pantry; no crush; climate awareness humidity.

Destination

Unpack hang immediately; air brief before closet; moth cedar optional not naphthalene direct contact silk.

Active Movers cultural textiles

Wardrobe boxes stock; tissue provided tier; hand-carry advise irreplaceable ceremony set.

Conclusion

Tibeb and traditional attire arrive ceremony-ready through garment-box zero-dust discipline—dusty corridor defeated at tissue layer not prayer at destination.
author: Active Movers Team
